research.title: Deploying Astro to Cloudflare Workers: Edge, D1, and R2 After the Acquisition
research.whyTrending: Post-acquisition, Astro on Cloudflare Workers gets first-class edge rendering plus D1 (database) and R2 (storage) integration. Timely, practical, and ties the #1 acquisition story to a concrete build.

seo.suggestedTitle: Deploying Astro to Cloudflare Workers: Edge, D1 & R2

gaps: Many guides predate Astro 6 API changes (Astro.locals.runtime.env removed; cloudflare:workers env import)
Static vs server output confusion (need output:server for cookies/request)
The wrangler dev vs astro dev gotcha (bindings unavailable in astro dev) buried
Type generation (wrangler types) for bindings skipped
Tying it to the acquisition context (why this is first-class now) missing

officialSources.note: @astrojs/cloudflare adapter, runtime bindings via context.locals / cloudflare:workers

expertInsights: @astrojs/cloudflare is the only officially supported way to run Astro SSR on Cloudflare; needs Astro 6 (adapter 13.6.0 peers astro ^6.3.0, wrangler ^4.83.0)
Set output: server for edge-side dynamic features (Astro.cookies, Astro.request); static export misses them
Bindings (KV, D1, R2) declared in wrangler.jsonc/toml; .dev.vars local, dashboard env for prod
Astro 6 removed Astro.locals.runtime.env; access bindings via import { env } from cloudflare:workers, or context.locals runtime in middleware
CRITICAL gotcha: astro dev does NOT have bindings - use wrangler dev to emulate KV/D1/R2 locally
Generate binding types with wrangler types + update env.d.ts
D1 (SQLite at edge) pairs well with Drizzle for a full-stack Astro app; R2 for object storage
Post-acquisition this is first-class - the reason to consider it now
commonProblems: Following pre-Astro-6 guides using removed Astro.locals.runtime.env
Testing bindings under astro dev (they arent there) instead of wrangler dev
static output when you need server for cookies/request

strategy.angle: A current (Astro 6 + post-acquisition) deploy guide that gets the API right where older tutorials are wrong: output:server, @astrojs/cloudflare adapter, bindings via cloudflare:workers env (not the removed Astro.locals.runtime.env), and the single biggest gotcha - astro dev has no bindings, use wrangler dev. Tie D1 and R2 to a concrete full-stack shape and connect to the acquisition story
strategy.title: Deploying Astro to Cloudflare Workers: Edge, D1, and R2 in 2026
strategy.excerpt: Post-acquisition, Astro on Cloudflare Workers is first-class - edge rendering plus D1 and R2 at your fingertips. But Astro 6 changed the bindings API and older guides will steer you wrong. Heres the current setup, the D1/R2 wiring, and the wrangler-dev gotcha that trips everyone.
hooks: Timely: after the Cloudflare acquisition, deploying Astro to Workers is the first-class path - heres how to actually do it
Gotcha: astro dev cannot see your D1 or R2 bindings - test with wrangler dev or chase phantom bugs
Warning: Astro 6 removed Astro.locals.runtime.env, so half the tutorials you find are already wrong
outline: Hook: first-class edge, if you get the setup right
Bindings: KV, D1, R2 in wrangler config
A full-stack shape: D1 + Drizzle, R2 for files
Why this is the path post-acquisition
